vcs.owner.name.description: The group owner within the version control system

vcs.provider.name.description: The name of the version control system provider

vcs.change.state.description: The state of a change (pull request)

vcs.line_change.type.description: The type of line change being measured on a ref (branch).

vcs.ref.base.name.description: The name of the VCS base reference (branch/tag) for comparison.

vcs.ref.base.type.description: The type of the base reference (branch, tag).

vcs.ref.head.name.description: The name of the VCS head reference (branch).

vcs.ref.head.type.description: The type of the head reference (branch, tag).

vcs.ref.type.description: The type of the reference in the repository.

vcs.repository.name.description: The name of the VCS repository.

vcs.repository.url.full.description: The canonical URL of the repository providing the complete HTTPS address.

vcs.change.count.description: The number of changes (pull requests) in a repository, categorized by their state (either open or merged).

vcs.change.duration.description: The time duration a change (pull request/merge request/changelist) has been in an open state.

vcs.change.time_to_approval.description: The amount of time it took a change (pull request) to go from open to approved.

vcs.change.time_to_merge.description: The amount of time it took a change (pull request) to go from open to merged.

vcs.contributor.count.description: The number of unique contributors to a repository.

vcs.ref.count.description: The number of refs of type branch in a repository.

vcs.ref.lines_delta.description: The number of lines added/removed in a ref (branch) relative to the default branch (trunk).

vcs.ref.revisions_delta.description: The number of revisions (commits) a ref (branch) is ahead/behind the branch from trunk (default).

vcs.ref.time.description: Time a ref (branch) created from the default branch (trunk) has existed. The `vcs.ref.type` attribute will always be `branch`.

vcs.repository.count.description: The number of repositories in an organization.

schema.description: Config that is exposed to this github receiver through the OTEL config.yaml

components.description: Workflow tracing support is actively being added to the GitLab receiver. This is accomplished through the processing of GitLab webhook events for pipelines. The [`pipeline`](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/integrations/webhook_events.html#pipeline-events) event payloads are then constructed into `trace` telemetry.

include_user_attributes.description: IncludeUserAttributes controls whether user information (commit author, pipeline actor) is included Default: false (user information is excluded by default for privacy)

schema.description: Config that is exposed to this gitlab receiver through the OTEL config.yaml

components.description: This receiver gets GCP (Google Cloud Platform) metrics from [GCP Monitoring REST API] via the [Google SDK for GCP Metrics] and then convert those timeseries data into OTel Format [Pipeline Data].

metadata.description:
The primary objective of the Google Cloud Monitoring Receiver is to gather time series metrics data from all Google
services and convert this data into a pipeline format that facilitates further use.

metric_descriptor_filter.description: Filter for listing metric descriptors. Only support `project` and `metric.type` as filter objects. See https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/api/v3/filters#metric-descriptor-filter for more details.

components.description: The following configuration options are supported:

metadata.description: This receiver gets OTLP messages from a Google Cloud [Pubsub](https://cloud.google.com/pubsub) subscription.

receiver.googlecloudpubsub.encoding_erro.description: Number of times a message couldn't be decoded by the configured encoder
receiver.googlecloudpubsub.encoding_erro.extended_documentation:
The receiver reads messages from Google Cloud Pub/Sub and tries to decode the message using the configured
encoder. Each time a message fails to decoded the counter is increased.

receiver.googlecloudpubsub.stream_restar.description: Number of times the stream (re)starts due to a Pub/Sub servers connection close
receiver.googlecloudpubsub.stream_restar.extended_documentation:
The receiver uses the Google Cloud Pub/Sub StreamingPull API and keeps a open connection. The Pub/Sub servers
recurrently close the connection after a time period to avoid a long-running sticky connection. This metric
counts the number of the resets that occurred during the lifetime of the container.

flow_control_config.description: FlowControlConfig defines the flow control configuration for the receiver. This is used to tune the internal flow control implementation, along with the Pub/Sub flow control settings documented at https://cloud.google.com/pubsub/docs/flow-control and https://cloud.google.com/pubsub/docs/reference/rpc/google.pubsub.v1#streamingpullrequest

max_outstanding_bytes.description: Pub/Sub flow control settings for the maximum number of outstanding bytes.

max_outstanding_messages.description: Pub/Sub flow control settings for the maximum number of outstanding messages.

stream_ack_deadline.description: The ack deadline to use for the Pub/Sub stream.

trigger_ack_batch_duration.description: The maximum duration the acknowledgement loop waits before sending the acknowledgements.

client_id.description: The client id that will be used by Pubsub to make load balancing decisions

compression.description: Lock down the compression of the payload, leave empty for attribute based detection

encoding.description: Lock down the encoding of the payload, leave empty for attribute based detection

endpoint.description: Override of the Pubsub Endpoint, leave empty for the default endpoint

ignore_encoding_error.description: Ignore errors when the configured encoder fails to decoding a PubSub messages

insecure.description: Only has effect if Endpoint is not ""

project.description: Google Cloud Project ID where the Pubsub client will connect to

subscription.description: The fully qualified resource name of the Pubsub subscription

user_agent.description: User agent that will be used by the Pubsub client to connect to the service

components.description: Google Cloud Spanner enable you to investigate issues with your database by exposing via [Total and Top N built-in tables](https://cloud.google.com/spanner/docs/introspection): - Query statistics - Read statistics - Transaction statistics - Lock statistics - and others
Path to the endpoint exposed by HAProxy for communications. It can be a local file socket or a HTTP URL.

metadata.description: The HAProxy receiver generates metrics by polling periodically the HAProxy process through a dedicated socket or HTTP URL.

haproxy.addr.description: address:port or "unix". IPv6 has brackets around the address.

haproxy.server.state.description: Server status (UP, DOWN, NOLB, MAINT, no check, etc.). Corresponds to HAProxy's `status` field.

haproxy.service_name.description: Service name (FRONTEND for frontend, BACKEND for backend, any name for server/listener)

haproxy.active.description: Number of active servers (backend) or server is active (server). Corresponds to HAProxy's `act` metric.

haproxy.backup.description: Number of backup servers (backend) or server is backup (server). Corresponds to HAProxy's `bck` metric.

haproxy.bytes.input.description: Bytes in. Corresponds to HAProxy's `bin` metric.

haproxy.bytes.output.description: Bytes out. Corresponds to HAProxy's `bout` metric.

haproxy.clients.canceled.description: Number of data transfers aborted by the client. Corresponds to HAProxy's `cli_abrt` metric

haproxy.compression.bypass.description: Number of bytes that bypassed the HTTP compressor (CPU/BW limit). Corresponds to HAProxy's `comp_byp` metric.

haproxy.compression.count.description: Number of HTTP responses that were compressed. Corresponds to HAProxy's `comp_rsp` metric.

haproxy.compression.input.description: Number of HTTP response bytes fed to the compressor. Corresponds to HAProxy's `comp_in` metric.

haproxy.compression.output.description: Number of HTTP response bytes emitted by the compressor. Corresponds to HAProxy's `comp_out` metric.

haproxy.connections.average_time.description: Average connect time in ms over the 1024 last requests. Corresponds to HAProxy's `ctime` metric.

haproxy.connections.errors.description: Number of requests that encountered an error trying to connect to a backend server. The backend stat is the sum of the stat. Corresponds to HAProxy's `econ` metric

haproxy.connections.rate.description: Number of connections over the last elapsed second (frontend). Corresponds to HAProxy's `conn_rate` metric.

haproxy.connections.retries.description: Number of times a connection to a server was retried. Corresponds to HAProxy's `wretr` metric.

haproxy.connections.total.description: Cumulative number of connections (frontend). Corresponds to HAProxy's `conn_tot` metric.

haproxy.downtime.description: Total downtime (in seconds). The value for the backend is the downtime for the whole backend, not the sum of the server downtime. Corresponds to HAProxy's `downtime` metric

haproxy.failed_checks.description: Number of failed checks. (Only counts checks failed when the server is up). Corresponds to HAProxy's `chkfail` metric.

haproxy.requests.average_time.description: Average queue time in ms over the 1024 last requests. Corresponds to HAProxy's `qtime` metric.

haproxy.requests.denied.description: Requests denied because of security concerns. Corresponds to HAProxy's `dreq` metric

haproxy.requests.errors.description: Cumulative number of request errors. Corresponds to HAProxy's `ereq` metric.

haproxy.requests.queued.description: Current queued requests. For the backend this reports the number queued without a server assigned. Corresponds to HAProxy's `qcur` metric.

haproxy.requests.rate.description: HTTP requests per second over last elapsed second. Corresponds to HAProxy's `req_rate` metric.

haproxy.requests.redispatched.description: Number of times a request was redispatched to another server. Corresponds to HAProxy's `wredis` metric.

haproxy.requests.total.description: Total number of HTTP requests received. Corresponds to HAProxy's `req_tot`, `hrsp_1xx`, `hrsp_2xx`, `hrsp_3xx`, `hrsp_4xx`, `hrsp_5xx` and `hrsp_other` metrics.

haproxy.responses.average_time.description: Average response time in ms over the 1024 last requests. Corresponds to HAProxy's `rtime` metric.

haproxy.responses.denied.description: Responses denied because of security concerns. Corresponds to HAProxy's `dresp` metric

haproxy.responses.errors.description: Cumulative number of response errors. Corresponds to HAProxy's `eresp` metric, `srv_abrt` will be counted here also.

haproxy.server_selected.total.description: Number of times a server was selected, either for new sessions or when re-dispatching. Corresponds to HAProxy's `lbtot` metric.

haproxy.sessions.average.description: Average total session time in ms over the last 1024 requests. Corresponds to HAProxy's `ttime` metric.

haproxy.sessions.count.description: Current sessions. Corresponds to HAProxy's `scur` metric.

haproxy.sessions.limit.description: Configured session limit. Corresponds to HAProxy's `slim` metric.

haproxy.sessions.rate.description: Number of sessions per second over last elapsed second. Corresponds to HAProxy's `rate` metric.

haproxy.sessions.total.description: Cumulative number of sessions. Corresponds to HAProxy's `stot` metric.

haproxy.weight.description: Total effective weight (backend) or effective weight (server). Corresponds to HAProxy's `weight` metric.

components.description: The collection interval, root path, the categories of metrics, and individual metrics to be scraped can be configured:

metadata.description:
The Host Metrics receiver generates metrics about the host system scraped
from various sources and host entity event as log. This is intended to be
used when the collector is deployed as an agent.

feature_gates.description: When enabled, all process scrapes will use the boot time value that is cached at the start of the process.
When enabled, the used value for the system.memory.usage and system.memory.utilization metrics will be based on the Linux kernel's MemAvailable statistic instead of MemFree, Buffers, and Cached.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for HostMetrics receiver.

metadata_collection_interval.description: Collection interval for metadata. Metadata of the particular entity is collected when the entity changes. In addition metadata of all entities is collected periodically even if no changes happen. Setting the duration to 0 will disable periodic collection (however will not impact metadata collection on changes).

root_path.description: RootPath is the host's root directory (linux only).

components.description: The collection interval, root path, the categories of metrics, and individual metrics to be scraped can be configured:

metadata.description:
The Host Metrics receiver generates metrics about the host system scraped
from various sources and host entity event as log. This is intended to be
used when the collector is deployed as an agent.

feature_gates.description: When enabled, all process scrapes will use the boot time value that is cached at the start of the process.
When enabled, the used value for the system.memory.usage and system.memory.utilization metrics will be based on the Linux kernel's MemAvailable statistic instead of MemFree, Buffers, and Cached.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for HostMetrics receiver.

metadata_collection_interval.description: Collection interval for metadata. Metadata of the particular entity is collected when the entity changes. In addition metadata of all entities is collected periodically even if no changes happen. Setting the duration to 0 will disable periodic collection (however will not impact metadata collection on changes).

root_path.description: RootPath is the host's root directory (linux only).

components.description: This receiver will make a request to the specified `endpoint` using the configured `method`. This scraper generates a metric with a label for each HTTP response status class with a value of `1` if the status code matches the class. For example, the following metrics will be generated if the endpoint returned a `200`:

metadata.description: The HTTP Check Receiver can be used for synthetic checks against HTTP endpoints.

http.tls.cn.description: The commonName in the subject of the certificate.

http.tls.issuer.description: The entity that issued the certificate.

http.tls.san.description: The Subject Alternative Name of the certificate.

network.transport.description: OSI transport layer or inter-process communication method.

validation.type.description: Type of validation performed (contains, json_path, size, regex)

httpcheck.client.connection.duration.description: Time spent establishing TCP connection to the endpoint.

httpcheck.client.request.duration.description: Time spent sending the HTTP request to the endpoint.

httpcheck.dns.lookup.duration.description: Time spent performing DNS lookup for the endpoint.

httpcheck.duration.description: Measures the duration of the HTTP check.

httpcheck.error.description: Records errors occurring during HTTP check.

httpcheck.response.duration.description: Time spent receiving the HTTP response from the endpoint.

httpcheck.response.size.description: Size of response body in bytes.

httpcheck.status.description: 1 if the check resulted in status_code matching the status_class, otherwise 0.

httpcheck.tls.cert_remaining.description: Time in seconds until certificate expiry, as specified by `NotAfter` field in the x.509 certificate. Negative values represent time in seconds since expiration.

httpcheck.tls.handshake.duration.description: Time spent performing TLS handshake with the endpoint.

httpcheck.validation.failed.description: Number of response validations that failed.

httpcheck.validation.passed.description: Number of response validations that passed.

metadata.description: The HTTP Check Receiver can be used for synthetic checks against HTTP endpoints.

http.tls.cn.description: The commonName in the subject of the certificate.

http.tls.issuer.description: The entity that issued the certificate.

schema.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

components.description: Write endpoints exist at `/write` (InfluxDB 1.x compatibility) and `/api/v2/write` (InfluxDB 2.x compatibility). Write query parameters `db`/`rp` (InfluxDB 1.x) and `org`/`bucket` (InfluxDB 2.x) are ignored. Write query parameter `precision` is optional, defaults to `ns`.

metadata.description: This receiver accepts metrics data as [InfluxDB Line Protocol](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v2.0/reference/syntax/line-protocol/).

schema.description: Config defines configuration for the InfluxDB receiver.

components.description: The following settings are optional:

metadata.description:
The Microsoft IIS Receiver grabs metrics about an IIS instance using the Windows Performance Counters. Because of
this, it is a Windows only receiver.

iis.application_pool.description: The application pool, which is associated with worker processes of one or more applications.

iis.site.description: The site of the web server.

request.description: The type of request sent by a client.

iis.application_pool.state.description: The current status of the application pool (1 - Uninitialized, 2 - Initialized, 3 - Running, 4 - Disabling, 5 - Disabled, 6 - Shutdown Pending, 7 - Delete Pending).

iis.application_pool.uptime.description: The application pools uptime period since the last restart.

iis.connection.attempt.count.description: Total number of attempts to connect to the server.

iis.network.blocked.description: Number of bytes blocked due to bandwidth throttling.

iis.network.io.description: Total amount of bytes sent and received.

iis.request.count.description: Total number of requests of a given type.

iis.request.queue.age.max.description: Age of oldest request in the queue.

iis.request.queue.count.description: Current number of requests in the queue.

iis.uptime.description: The amount of time the server has been up.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for simple prometheus receiver.

components.description: By default, the Jaeger receiver will not serve any protocol. A protocol must be named under the `protocols` object for the jaeger receiver to start. The below protocols are supported, each supports an optional `endpoint` object configuration parameter.

metadata.description: The Jaeger Receiver receives trace data in the [Jaeger](https://www.jaegertracing.io/) format.

protocol_udp.description: ProtocolUDP is the configuration for a UDP protocol.

protocols.description: Protocols is the configuration for the supported protocols.

remote_sampling_config.description: RemoteSamplingConfig defines config key for remote sampling fetch endpoint

server_config_udp.description: ServerConfigUDP is the server configuration for a UDP protocol.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for Jaeger receiver.

components.description: The `jmxreceiver` component is deprecated. Running Java as part of the Collector introduces significant operational complexity, especially since the Collector is commonly deployed as a container and official images do not include a Java runtime. This results in a broken user experience.

additional_jars.description: Array of additional JARs to be added to the class path when launching the JMX Metric Gatherer JAR Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

endpoint.description: The Service URL or host:port for the target coerced to one of form: service:jmx:rmi:///jndi/rmi://<host>:<port>/jmxrmi. Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

jar_path.description: The path for the JMX Metric Gatherer or JMX Scraper JAR (/opt/opentelemetry-java-contrib-jmx-metrics.jar by default). Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

jmx_configs.description: Comma-separated list of paths to custom YAML metrics definition, mandatory when TargetSystem is not set. Supported by: jmx-scraper

keystore_password.description: The keystore password for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

keystore_path.description: The keystore path for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

keystore_type.description: The keystore type for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

log_level.description: Log level used by the JMX metric gatherer. Should be one of: `"trace"`, `"debug"`, `"info"`, `"warn"`, `"error"`, `"off"` Supported by: jmx-metric-gatherer

otlp.description: The OTLP exporter settings Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

endpoint.description: The OTLP Receiver endpoint to send metrics to ("0.0.0.0:<random open port>" by default).

headers.description: The headers to include in OTLP metric submission requests.

password.description: The JMX password Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

realm.description: The SASL/DIGEST-MD5 realm Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

remote_profile.description: The JMX remote profile.  Should be one of: `"SASL/PLAIN"`, `"SASL/DIGEST-MD5"`, `"SASL/CRAM-MD5"`, `"TLS SASL/PLAIN"`, `"TLS SASL/DIGEST-MD5"`, or `"TLS SASL/CRAM-MD5"`, though no enforcement is applied. Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

resource_attributes.description: Map of resource attributes used by the Java SDK Autoconfigure to set resource attributes Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

target_source.description: The target source of metric definitions to use for the target system. Supported values are: auto, instrumentation and legacy. Supported by: jmx-scraper

target_system.description: Comma-separated list of systems to monitor Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

truststore_password.description: The truststore password for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

truststore_path.description: The truststore path for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

truststore_type.description: The truststore type for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

username.description: The JMX username Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

metadata.description:
The Kubernetes Cluster receiver collects cluster-level metrics and entity events from the Kubernetes
API server. It uses the K8s API to listen for updates. A single instance of this
receiver should be used to monitor a cluster.

container.runtime.description: The container runtime used by Kubernetes Node.

container.runtime.version.description: The version of container runtime used by Kubernetes Node.

k8s.container.status.last_terminated_rea.description: Last terminated reason of a container.

k8s.hpa.scaletargetref.apiversion.description: The API version of the target resource to scale for the HorizontalPodAutoscaler.

k8s.hpa.scaletargetref.kind.description: The kind of the target resource to scale for the HorizontalPodAutoscaler.

k8s.hpa.scaletargetref.name.description: The name of the target resource to scale for the HorizontalPodAutoscaler.

k8s.kubelet.version.description: The version of Kubelet running on the node.

k8s.pod.qos_class.description: The k8s pod qos class name. One of Guaranteed, Burstable, BestEffort.

k8s.service.publish_not_ready_addresses.description: Whether the Service publishes not-ready endpoints.

k8s.service.traffic_distribution.description: The traffic distribution policy for the Service (e.g., PreferClose).

os.description.description: The os description used by Kubernetes Node.

os.type.description: The os type used by Kubernetes Node.

condition.description: the name of Kubernetes Node condition. Example: Ready, Memory, PID, DiskPressure

k8s.container.status.reason.description: The reason of the current container status.

k8s.container.status.state.description: The state of the container (terminated, running, waiting). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.30/#containerstate-v1-core for details.

k8s.service.endpoint.address_type.description: The address type of the endpoint.

k8s.service.endpoint.condition.description: The condition of the service endpoint.

k8s.service.endpoint.zone.description: The zone of the service endpoint, typically corresponding to a failure domain.

resource.description: the name of the resource on which the quota is applied

k8s.container.cpu_limit.description: Maximum resource limit set for the container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.23/#resourcerequirements-v1-core for details

k8s.container.cpu_request.description: Resource requested for the container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.23/#resourcerequirements-v1-core for details

k8s.container.ephemeralstorage_limit.description: Maximum resource limit set for the container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.23/#resourcerequirements-v1-core for details

k8s.container.ephemeralstorage_request.description: Resource requested for the container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.23/#resourcerequirements-v1-core for details

k8s.container.memory_limit.description: Maximum resource limit set for the container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.23/#resourcerequirements-v1-core for details

k8s.container.memory_request.description: Resource requested for the container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.23/#resourcerequirements-v1-core for details

k8s.container.ready.description: Whether a container has passed its readiness probe (0 for no, 1 for yes)

k8s.container.restarts.description: How many times the container has restarted in the recent past. This value is pulled directly from the K8s API and the value can go indefinitely high and be reset to 0 at any time depending on how your kubelet is configured to prune dead containers. It is best to not depend too much on the exact value but rather look at it as either == 0, in which case you can conclude there were no restarts in the recent past, or > 0, in which case you can conclude there were restarts in the recent past, and not try and analyze the value beyond that.

k8s.container.status.reason.description: Experimental metric, may experience breaking changes. Describes the number of K8s containers that are currently in a state for a given reason. All possible container state reasons will be reported at each time interval to avoid missing metrics. Only the value corresponding to the current state reason will be non-zero.

k8s.container.status.state.description: Experimental metric, may experience breaking changes. Describes the number of K8s containers that are currently in a given state. All possible container states will be reported at each time interval to avoid missing metrics. Only the value corresponding to the current state will be non-zero.

k8s.container.storage_limit.description: Maximum resource limit set for the container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.23/#resourcerequirements-v1-core for details

k8s.container.storage_request.description: Resource requested for the container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.23/#resourcerequirements-v1-core for details

k8s.cronjob.active_jobs.description: The number of actively running jobs for a cronjob

k8s.daemonset.current_scheduled_nodes.description: Number of nodes that are running at least 1 daemon pod and are supposed to run the daemon pod

k8s.daemonset.desired_scheduled_nodes.description: Number of nodes that should be running the daemon pod (including nodes currently running the daemon pod)

k8s.daemonset.misscheduled_nodes.description: Number of nodes that are running the daemon pod, but are not supposed to run the daemon pod

k8s.daemonset.ready_nodes.description: Number of nodes that should be running the daemon pod and have one or more of the daemon pod running and ready

k8s.deployment.available.description: Total number of available pods (ready for at least minReadySeconds) targeted by this deployment

k8s.deployment.desired.description: Number of desired pods in this deployment

k8s.hpa.current_replicas.description: Current number of pod replicas managed by this autoscaler.

k8s.hpa.desired_replicas.description: Desired number of pod replicas managed by this autoscaler.

k8s.hpa.max_replicas.description: Maximum number of replicas to which the autoscaler can scale up.

k8s.hpa.min_replicas.description: Minimum number of replicas to which the autoscaler can scale up.

k8s.job.active_pods.description: The number of actively running pods for a job

k8s.job.desired_successful_pods.description: The desired number of successfully finished pods the job should be run with

k8s.job.failed_pods.description: The number of pods which reached phase Failed for a job

k8s.job.max_parallel_pods.description: The max desired number of pods the job should run at any given time

k8s.job.successful_pods.description: The number of pods which reached phase Succeeded for a job

k8s.namespace.phase.description: The current phase of namespaces (1 for active and 0 for terminating)

k8s.node.condition.description: The condition of a particular Node.

k8s.pod.phase.description: Current phase of the pod (1 - Pending, 2 - Running, 3 - Succeeded, 4 - Failed, 5 - Unknown)

k8s.pod.status_reason.description: Current status reason of the pod (1 - Evicted, 2 - NodeAffinity, 3 - NodeLost, 4 - Shutdown, 5 - UnexpectedAdmissionError, 6 - Unknown)

k8s.replicaset.available.description: Total number of available pods (ready for at least minReadySeconds) targeted by this replicaset

k8s.replicaset.desired.description: Number of desired pods in this replicaset

k8s.replication_controller.available.description: Total number of available pods (ready for at least minReadySeconds) targeted by this replication_controller

k8s.replication_controller.desired.description: Number of desired pods in this replication_controller

k8s.resource_quota.hard_limit.description: The upper limit for a particular resource in a specific namespace. Will only be sent if a quota is specified. CPU requests/limits will be sent as millicores

k8s.resource_quota.used.description: The usage for a particular resource in a specific namespace. Will only be sent if a quota is specified. CPU requests/limits will be sent as millicores

k8s.service.endpoint.count.description: The number of endpoints for a service, broken down by condition, address type, and zone.

k8s.service.load_balancer.ingress.count.description: The number of load balancer ingress points (external IPs/hostnames) assigned to the service.

k8s.statefulset.current_pods.description: The number of pods created by the StatefulSet controller from the StatefulSet version

k8s.statefulset.desired_pods.description: Number of desired pods in the stateful set (the `spec.replicas` field)

k8s.statefulset.ready_pods.description: Number of pods created by the stateful set that have the `Ready` condition

k8s.statefulset.updated_pods.description: Number of pods created by the StatefulSet controller from the StatefulSet version

openshift.appliedclusterquota.limit.description: The upper limit for a particular resource in a specific namespace.

openshift.appliedclusterquota.used.description: The usage for a particular resource in a specific namespace.

openshift.clusterquota.limit.description: The configured upper limit for a particular resource.

openshift.clusterquota.used.description: The usage for a particular resource with a configured limit.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for kubernetes cluster receiver.

allocatable_types_to_report.description: Allocate resource types to report. See all resource types, see here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/reserve-compute-resources/#node-allocatable

distribution.description: Whether OpenShift support should be enabled or not.

k8s_leader_elector.description: K8sLeaderElector defines the reference to the k8s leader elector extension use this when k8s cluster receiver needs to be deployed in HA mode

metadata_collection_interval.description: Collection interval for metadata. Metadata of the particular entity in the cluster is collected when the entity changes. In addition metadata of all entities is collected periodically even if no changes happen. Setting the duration to 0 will disable periodic collection (however will not impact metadata collection on changes).

metadata_exporters.description: List of exporters to which metadata from this receiver should be forwarded to.

namespace.description: Deprecated: This field is no longer supported, use cfg.Namespaces instead.

namespaces.description: Namespaces to fetch resources from. If this is set, certain cluster-wide resources such as Nodes or Namespaces will not be able to be observed. Setting this option is recommended in environments where due to security restrictions the collector cannot be granted cluster-wide permissions.

node_conditions_to_report.description: Node condition types to report. See all condition types, see here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/architecture/nodes/#condition.

components.description: Currently this receiver supports authentication via service accounts only. See [example](#example) for more information.

metadata.description:
The Kubernetes Events Receiver collects events from the Kubernetes
API server. It collects all the new or updated events that come in.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for kubernetes events receiver.

namespaces.description: List of ‘namespaces’ to collect events from.

components.description: Currently this receiver supports authentication via service accounts only. See [example](#example) for more information.

metadata.description: The Kubernetes Objects Receiver collects (pull/watch) objects from the Kubernetes API server.

components.description: This receiver supports Kafka versions: - 2.X - 3.X

metadata.description:
The Kafka Metrics Receiver collects kafka metrics (brokers, topics, partitions, consumer groups) from a kafka server,
and converts them into otlp.

feature_gates.description: When enabled, the Kafka Metrics receiver will use the franz-go client to connect to Kafka.

kafka.cluster.alias.description: The alias name (string) of the cluster

broker.description: The ID of the kafka broker

group.description: The ID (string) of a consumer group

kafka.broker.log_retention_period.description: log retention time (s) of a broker.

kafka.brokers.description: Number of brokers in the cluster.

kafka.consumer_group.lag.description: Current approximate lag of consumer group at partition of topic

kafka.consumer_group.lag_sum.description: Current approximate sum of consumer group lag across all partitions of topic

kafka.consumer_group.members.description: Count of members in the consumer group

kafka.consumer_group.offset.description: Current offset of the consumer group at partition of topic

kafka.consumer_group.offset_sum.description: Sum of consumer group offset across partitions of topic

kafka.partition.current_offset.description: Current offset of partition of topic.

kafka.partition.oldest_offset.description: Oldest offset of partition of topic

kafka.partition.replicas.description: Number of replicas for partition of topic

kafka.partition.replicas_in_sync.description: Number of synchronized replicas of partition

kafka.topic.log_retention_period.description: log retention period of a topic (s).

kafka.topic.log_retention_size.description: log retention size of a topic in Bytes, The value (-1) indicates infinite size.

kafka.topic.min_insync_replicas.description: minimum in-sync replicas of a topic.

schema.description: Config represents user settings for kafkametrics receiver

cluster_alias.description: Alias name of the kafka cluster

group_match.description: GroupMatch consumer groups to collect on

refresh_frequency.description: Cluster metadata refresh frequency Configures the refresh frequency to update cached cluster metadata Defaults to 10 minutes from Sarama library If Metadata.RefreshInterval is set, this will be ignored. Deprecated [v0.122.0]: use Metadata.RefreshInterval instead.

scrapers.description: Scrapers defines which metric data points to be captured from kafka

topic_match.description: TopicMatch topics to collect metrics on

components.description: There are no required settings.

metadata.description:
The Kafka Receiver receives telemetry data from Kafka, with configurable topics and encodings. If used in conjunction
with the `kafkaexporter` configured with `include_metadata_keys`, The Kafka Receiver will also propagate the Kafka
headers to the downstream pipeline, giving access to the rest of the pipeline to arbitrary metadata keys and values.

kafka_broker_closed.description: The total number of connections closed.
kafka_broker_closed.extended_documentation: Only produced when franz-go is enabled.

kafka_broker_connects.description: The total number of connections opened.
kafka_broker_connects.extended_documentation: Only produced when franz-go is enabled.

kafka_broker_throttling_duration.description: The throttling duration in ms imposed by the broker when receiving messages.
kafka_broker_throttling_duration.extended_documentation: Only produced when franz-go is enabled. Deprecated in favor of kafka_broker_throttling_latency.

warnings.if_enabled: This metric is deprecated in favor of kafka_broker_throttling_latency and will be removed soon.

kafka_broker_throttling_latency.description: The throttling latency in seconds imposed by the broker when receiving records.
kafka_broker_throttling_latency.extended_documentation: Only produced when franz-go is enabled.

kafka_receiver_bytes.description: The size in bytes of received records seen by the broker.
kafka_receiver_bytes.extended_documentation: Only produced when franz-go is enabled.

kafka_receiver_bytes_uncompressed.description: The uncompressed size in bytes of received records seen by the client.

kafka_receiver_latency.description: The time it took in ms to receive a batch of messages.
kafka_receiver_latency.extended_documentation: Only produced when franz-go is enabled. Deprecated in favor of kafka_receiver_read_latency.

warnings.if_enabled: This metric is deprecated in favor of kafka_receiver_read_latency and will be removed soon.
This metric is deprecated in favor of kafka_receiver_records and will be removed soon.

kafka_receiver_read_latency.description: The time it took in seconds to receive a batch of records.
kafka_receiver_read_latency.extended_documentation: Only produced when franz-go is enabled.

kafka_receiver_records_delay.description: The time in seconds between producing and receiving a batch of records.
kafka_receiver_records_delay.extended_documentation:
Optional, only reported when enabled via config.
Note that this metric may slow down high-volume consuming.
Only produced when franz-go is enabled.
This metric is reported with an assumption that the exporter and the receiver clocks are synchronized.

kafka_receiver_unmarshal_failed_log_reco.description: Number of log records failed to be unmarshaled

kafka_receiver_unmarshal_failed_metric_p.description: Number of metric points failed to be unmarshaled

kafka_receiver_unmarshal_failed_profiles.description: Number of profiles failed to be unmarshaled

kafka_receiver_unmarshal_failed_spans.description: Number of spans failed to be unmarshaled

after.description: If true, the messages are marked after the pipeline execution

on_error.description: If false, only the successfully processed messages are marked. This only applies to non-permanent errors. It has no impact if After is set to false. Note: this can block the entire partition in case a message processing returns a non-permanent error.

on_permanent_error.description: If false, only the successfully processed messages are marked. This only applies to permanent errors. It has no impact if After is set to false. Default value inherits from OnError for backward compatibility. Note: this can block the entire partition in case a message processing returns a permanent error.

metric_config.description: MetricConfig provides common config for a particular metric.

metrics_config.description: MetricsConfig provides config for optional receiver metrics.

kafka_receiver_records_delay.description: KafkaReceiverRecordsDelay controls whether the metric kafka_receiver_records_delay that measures the time in seconds between producing and receiving a batch of records will be reported or not. This metric is not reported by default because it may slow down high-volume consuming.

topic_encoding_config.description: TopicEncodingConfig holds signal-specific topic and encoding configuration.

encoding.description: Encoding holds the expected encoding of messages for the signal type Defaults to "otlp_proto".

exclude_topics.description: Optional exclude topics option, used only in regex mode.

topics.description: Topics holds the name of the Kafka topics from which messages of the signal type should be consumed. The default depends on the signal type: - "otlp_spans" for traces - "otlp_metrics" for metrics - "otlp_logs" for logs - "otlp_profiles" for profiles

schema.description: Config defines configuration for Kafka receiver.

error_backoff.description: ErrorBackoff controls backoff/retry behavior when the next consumer returns an error.

header_extraction.description: HeaderExtraction controls extraction of headers from Kafka records.

logs.description: Logs holds configuration about how logs should be consumed.

message_marking.description: MessageMarking controls the way the messages are marked as consumed.

metrics.description: Metrics holds configuration about how metrics should be consumed.

profiles.description: Profiles holds configuration about how profiles should be consumed.

traces.description: Traces holds configuration about how traces should be consumed.

components.description: Details about the metrics produced by this receiver can be found in [metadata.yaml](./metadata.yaml) with further documentation in [documentation.md](./documentation.md)

metadata.description:
The Kubelet Stats Receiver pulls node, pod, container, and volume metrics from the API server on a kubelet
and sends it down the metric pipeline for further processing.

k8s.persistentvolumeclaim.name.description: The name of the Persistent Volume Claim

direction.description: Direction of flow of bytes/operations (receive or transmit).

container.cpu.time.description: Total cumulative CPU time (sum of all cores) spent by the container/pod/node since its creation

container.cpu.usage.description: Total CPU usage (sum of all cores per second) averaged over the sample window

container.uptime.description: The time since the container started

k8s.container.cpu.node.utilization.description: Container cpu utilization as a ratio of the node's capacity

k8s.container.cpu_limit_utilization.description: Container cpu utilization as a ratio of the container's limits

k8s.container.cpu_request_utilization.description: Container cpu utilization as a ratio of the container's requests

k8s.container.memory.node.utilization.description: Container memory utilization as a ratio of the node's capacity

k8s.container.memory_limit_utilization.description: Container memory utilization as a ratio of the container's limits

k8s.container.memory_request_utilization.description: Container memory utilization as a ratio of the container's requests

k8s.node.cpu.time.description: Total cumulative CPU time (sum of all cores) spent by the container/pod/node since its creation

k8s.node.cpu.usage.description: Total CPU usage (sum of all cores per second) averaged over the sample window

k8s.node.uptime.description: The time since the node started

k8s.pod.cpu.node.utilization.description: Pod cpu utilization as a ratio of the node's capacity

k8s.pod.cpu.time.description: Total cumulative CPU time (sum of all cores) spent by the container/pod/node since its creation

k8s.pod.cpu.usage.description: Total CPU usage (sum of all cores per second) averaged over the sample window

k8s.pod.cpu_limit_utilization.description: Pod cpu utilization as a ratio of the pod's total container limits. If any container is missing a limit the metric is not emitted.

k8s.pod.cpu_request_utilization.description: Pod cpu utilization as a ratio of the pod's total container requests. If any container is missing a request the metric is not emitted.

k8s.pod.memory.node.utilization.description: Pod memory utilization as a ratio of the node's capacity

k8s.pod.memory_limit_utilization.description: Pod memory utilization as a ratio of the pod's total container limits. If any container is missing a limit the metric is not emitted.

k8s.pod.memory_request_utilization.description: Pod memory utilization as a ratio of the pod's total container requests. If any container is missing a request the metric is not emitted.

k8s.pod.uptime.description: The time since the pod started

k8s.pod.volume.usage.description: The number of used bytes in the pod volume.

k8s.volume.available.description: The number of available bytes in the volume.

k8s.volume.capacity.description: The total capacity in bytes of the volume.

k8s.volume.inodes.description: The total inodes in the filesystem.

k8s.volume.inodes.free.description: The free inodes in the filesystem.

k8s.volume.inodes.used.description: The inodes used by the filesystem. This may not equal inodes - free because filesystem may share inodes with other filesystems.

collect_all_network_interfaces.description: NetworkCollectAllInterfaces allows to enable collecting metrics from all network interfaces instead of default one Can be set separately for Pod and Node network metrics

extra_metadata_labels.description: ExtraMetadataLabels contains list of extra metadata that should be taken from /pods endpoint and put as extra labels on metrics resource. No additional metadata is fetched by default, so there are no extra calls to /pods endpoint. Supported values include container.id and k8s.volume.type.

k8s_api_config.description: Configuration of the Kubernetes API client.

metric_groups.description: MetricGroupsToCollect provides a list of metrics groups to collect metrics from. "container", "pod", "node" and "volume" are the only valid groups.

node.description: NodeName is the node name to limit the discovery of nodes. For example, node name can be set using the downward API inside the collector pod spec as follows: env: - name: K8S_NODE_NAME valueFrom: fieldRef: fieldPath: spec.nodeName Then set this value to ${env:K8S_NODE_NAME} in the configuration.

components.description: The configuration has 2 parts, One is the HTTP receiver configuration and the rest is about mapping attributes from the freeform libhoney format into the more structured OpenTelemetry objects.

metadata.description:
The Libhoney Receiver will accept Trace and Logs signals that are emitted from applications that are
instrumented using [Libhoney](https://docs.honeycomb.io/send-data/logs/structured/libhoney/) libraries.

http_config.description: HTTPConfig defines the configuration for the HTTP server receiving traces.

traces_url_paths.description: The URL path to receive traces on. If omitted "/" will be used.

schema.description: Config represents the receiver config settings within the collector's config.yaml

components.description: The settings are:

metadata.description:
The Loki receiver implements the [Loki push api](https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/clients/promtail/#loki-push-api)
as specified in [Loki HTTP API: Ingest logs](https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/reference/loki-http-api/#ingest-logs).
It allows Promtail instances to specify the open telemetry collector as their lokiAddress.

This receiver runs HTTP and GRPC servers to ingest log entries in Loki format.

protocols.description: Protocols is the configuration for the supported protocols.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for the lokireceiver receiver.

components.description: - macOS 10.12 (Sierra) or later - The `log` command must be available in PATH - For archive mode: Read access to the `.logarchive` directory - For live mode: Appropriate permissions to read system logs

metadata.description:
The macOS Unified Logging Receiver collects logs from macOS systems using the native `log` command. This receiver
supports both live system logs and archived log files (`.logarchive`).

schema.description: Config defines configuration for the macOS unified logging receiver Separated into a common file that isn't platform specific so that factory_others.go can reference it

archive_path.description: ArchivePath is a path or glob pattern to .logarchive directory(ies) If empty, reads from the live system logs Supports glob patterns (e.g., "*.logarchive", "**/logs/*.logarchive")

end_time.description: EndTime specifies when to stop reading logs Only used with archive_path

format.description: Format specifies the output format from the log command Options: "default" (system default), "ndjson", "json", "syslog", "compact" Default: "default"

max_log_age.description: MaxLogAge specifies the maximum age of logs to read on startup Only applies to live mode. Format: "24h", "1h30m", etc.

max_poll_interval.description: MaxPollInterval specifies the maximum interval between polling for new logs (live mode only) The actual poll interval uses exponential backoff based on whether logs are being actively written

predicate.description: Predicate is a filter predicate to pass to the log command Example: "subsystem == 'com.apple.systempreferences'"

start_time.description: StartTime specifies when to start reading logs from Format: "2006-01-02 15:04:05"

components.description: - macOS 10.12 (Sierra) or later - The `log` command must be available in PATH - For archive mode: Read access to the `.logarchive` directory - For live mode: Appropriate permissions to read system logs

metadata.description:
The macOS Unified Logging Receiver collects logs from macOS systems using the native `log` command. This receiver
supports both live system logs and archived log files (`.logarchive`).

schema.description: Config defines configuration for the macOS unified logging receiver Separated into a common file that isn't platform specific so that factory_others.go can reference it

archive_path.description: ArchivePath is a path or glob pattern to .logarchive directory(ies) If empty, reads from the live system logs Supports glob patterns (e.g., "*.logarchive", "**/logs/*.logarchive")

end_time.description: EndTime specifies when to stop reading logs Only used with archive_path

format.description: Format specifies the output format from the log command Options: "default" (system default), "ndjson", "json", "syslog", "compact" Default: "default"

max_log_age.description: MaxLogAge specifies the maximum age of logs to read on startup Only applies to live mode. Format: "24h", "1h30m", etc.

max_poll_interval.description: MaxPollInterval specifies the maximum interval between polling for new logs (live mode only) The actual poll interval uses exponential backoff based on whether logs are being actively written

predicate.description: Predicate is a filter predicate to pass to the log command Example: "subsystem == 'com.apple.systempreferences'"

start_time.description: StartTime specifies when to start reading logs from Format: "2006-01-02 15:04:05"

components.description: The following settings are required:

metadata.description:
This receiver can fetch stats from a Memcached instance using the [stats
command](https://github.com/memcached/memcached/wiki/Commands#statistics). A
detailed description of all the stats available is at
https://github.com/memcached/memcached/blob/master/doc/protocol.txt#L1159.

memcached.bytes.description: Current number of bytes used by this server to store items.

memcached.connections.current.description: The current number of open connections.

memcached.connections.total.description: Total number of connections opened since the server started running.

memcached.current_items.description: Number of items currently stored in the cache.

memcached.operation_hit_ratio.description: Hit ratio for operations, expressed as a percentage value between 0.0 and 100.0.

memcached.threads.description: Number of threads used by the memcached instance.

components.description: The MongoDB Atlas receiver takes the following parameters. `public_key` and `private_key` are the only two required values to receive metrics and logs and are obtained via the "API Keys" tab of the MongoDB Atlas Project Access Manager. In the example below both values are being pulled from the environment.

metadata.description:
The MongoDB Atlas Receiver receives metrics from [MongoDB Atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/cloud/atlas) 
via their [monitoring APIs](https://docs.atlas.mongodb.com/reference/api/monitoring-and-logs/),
as well as alerts via a configured [webhook](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/tutorial/third-party-service-integrations/)
and events from [events APIs](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/reference/api/events/).
