projects.description: these parameters are only relevant in retrieval mode

events_config.description: EventsConfig is the configuration options for events collection

components.description: The purpose of this receiver is to allow users to monitor metrics from standalone MongoDB clusters. This includes non-Atlas managed MongoDB Servers.

metadata.description:
This receiver fetches stats from a MongoDB instance using the 
[golang mongo driver](https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver). Stats are collected via MongoDB's `dbStats` and
`serverStatus` commands.

server.address.description: The address of the MongoDB host.

server.port.description: The port of the MongoDB host.

service.instance.id.description: A unique identifier of the MongoDB resource as a UUID v5, derived from server address and port.

lock_mode.description: The mode of Lock which denotes the degree of access

lock_type.description: The Resource over which the Lock controls access

operation_latency.description: The MongoDB operation with regards to latency

type.description: The result of a cache request.

mongodb.active.reads.description: The number of read operations currently being processed.

mongodb.active.writes.description: The number of write operations currently being processed.

mongodb.cache.operations.description: The number of cache operations of the instance.

mongodb.commands.rate.description: The number of commands executed per second.

mongodb.cursor.count.description: The number of open cursors maintained for clients.

mongodb.cursor.timeout.count.description: The number of cursors that have timed out.

mongodb.data.size.description: The size of the collection. Data compression does not affect this value.

mongodb.deletes.rate.description: The number of deletes executed per second.

mongodb.document.operation.count.description: The number of document operations executed.

mongodb.flushes.rate.description: The number of flushes executed per second.

mongodb.getmores.rate.description: The number of getmores executed per second.

mongodb.global_lock.time.description: The time the global lock has been held.

mongodb.health.description: The health status of the server.
mongodb.health.extended_documentation: A value of '1' indicates healthy. A value of '0' indicates unhealthy.

mongodb.index.access.count.description: The number of times an index has been accessed.

mongodb.index.size.description: Sum of the space allocated to all indexes in the database, including free index space.

mongodb.inserts.rate.description: The number of insertions executed per second.

mongodb.lock.acquire.count.description: Number of times the lock was acquired in the specified mode.

mongodb.lock.acquire.time.description: Cumulative wait time for the lock acquisitions.

mongodb.lock.acquire.wait_count.description: Number of times the lock acquisitions encountered waits because the locks were held in a conflicting mode.

mongodb.lock.deadlock.count.description: Number of times the lock acquisitions encountered deadlocks.

mongodb.network.request.count.description: The number of requests received by the server.

mongodb.operation.repl.count.description: The number of replicated operations executed.

mongodb.operation.time.description: The total time spent performing operations.

mongodb.queries.rate.description: The number of queries executed per second.

mongodb.repl_commands_per_sec.description: The number of replicated commands executed per second.

mongodb.repl_deletes_per_sec.description: The number of replicated deletes executed per second.

mongodb.repl_getmores_per_sec.description: The number of replicated getmores executed per second.

mongodb.repl_inserts_per_sec.description: The number of replicated insertions executed per second.

mongodb.repl_queries_per_sec.description: The number of replicated queries executed per second.

mongodb.repl_updates_per_sec.description: The number of replicated updates executed per second.

mongodb.session.count.description: The total number of active sessions.

mongodb.storage.size.description: The total amount of storage allocated to this collection.
mongodb.storage.size.extended_documentation: If collection data is compressed it reflects the compressed size.

mongodb.updates.rate.description: The number of updates executed per second.

mongodb.uptime.description: The amount of time that the server has been running.

mongodb.wtcache.bytes.read.description: The number of bytes read into the WiredTiger cache.

hosts.description: Deprecated - Transport option will be removed in v0.102.0

components.description: This receiver queries MySQL's global status and InnoDB tables.

buffer_pool_data.description: The status of buffer pool data.

client.address.description: Hostname or address of the client.

client.port.description: TCP port used by the client.

db.namespace.description: The default database for the thread, or empty if none has been selected (originally processlist_db).

db.query.text.description: The SQL statement text for the event.

db.system.name.description: The name of the database system.

direction.description: The name of the transmission direction.

log_operations.description: The log operation types. 'fsyncs' aren't available in MariaDB 10.8 or later.

mysql.event_id.description: The thread associated with the event and the thread current event number when the event starts.

mysql.events_statements_current.digest.description: The statement digest SHA-256 value as a string of 64 hexadecimal characters, or empty if the statements_digest consumer is no.

mysql.events_statements_summary_by_diges.description: The number of times the statement was executed, report in delta value.
The statement digest SHA-256 value as a string of 64 hexadecimal characters, or empty if the statements_digest consumer is no.
The total time spent executing the statement, report in delta seconds.

mysql.events_waits_current.timer_wait.description: Timing information for the event, indicating elapsed time the event waited in seconds.

mysql.query_plan.description: The query plan for the statement, if available.

mysql.query_plan.hash.description: This attribute is set to the same value as mysql.events_statements_summary_by_digest.digest (query digest) by design.

mysql.session.id.description: The unique identifier for the session associated with the event.

mysql.threads.processlist_command.description: The type of command the thread is executing on behalf of the client for foreground threads, or `Sleep` if the session is idle.

mysql.threads.processlist_state.description: An action, event, or state that indicates what the thread is doing.

mysql.threads.thread_id.description: The unique identifier for the thread executing the statement.

mysql.wait_type.description: The name of the instrument that produced the event.

network.peer.address.description: IP address of the peer client.

network.peer.port.description: TCP port used by the peer client.

schema.description: The schema of the object.

table_name.description: Table name for event or process.

user.name.description: The user associated with a foreground thread, empty for a background thread (originally processlist_user).

db.server.query_sample.description:
Query sample collection enables monitoring of current running database statements.
This provides real-time visibility into active queries, helping users monitor database activity and performance as part of their observability pipeline.

db.server.top_query.description:
Top query collection enables monitoring of the queries that consumed the most CPU in the database.
This provides insights into query performance and resource usage, helping users identify and optimize high-impact queries as part of their observability pipeline.

mysql.buffer_pool.data_pages.description: The number of data pages in the InnoDB buffer pool.

mysql.buffer_pool.limit.description: The configured size of the InnoDB buffer pool.

mysql.buffer_pool.operations.description: The number of operations on the InnoDB buffer pool.

mysql.buffer_pool.page_flushes.description: The number of requests to flush pages from the InnoDB buffer pool.

mysql.buffer_pool.pages.description: The number of pages in the InnoDB buffer pool.

mysql.buffer_pool.usage.description: The number of bytes in the InnoDB buffer pool.

mysql.client.network.io.description: The number of transmitted bytes between server and clients.

mysql.commands.description: The number of times each type of command has been executed.

mysql.connection.count.description: The number of connection attempts (successful or not) to the MySQL server.

mysql.connection.errors.description: Errors that occur during the client connection process.

mysql.double_writes.description: The number of writes to the InnoDB doublewrite buffer.

mysql.handlers.description: The number of requests to various MySQL handlers.

mysql.index.io.wait.count.description: The total count of I/O wait events for an index.

mysql.index.io.wait.time.description: The total time of I/O wait events for an index.

mysql.joins.description: The number of joins that perform table scans.

mysql.log_operations.description: The number of InnoDB log operations.

mysql.max_used_connections.description: Maximum number of connections used simultaneously since the server started.

mysql.mysqlx_connections.extended_documentation: This metric is specific for MySQL working as Document Store (X-Plugin). [more docs](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/document-store.html)

mysql.mysqlx_worker_threads.description: The number of worker threads available.
mysql.mysqlx_worker_threads.extended_documentation: This metric is specific for MySQL working as Document Store (X-Plugin). [more docs](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/document-store.html)

mysql.page_operations.description: The number of InnoDB page operations.

mysql.prepared_statements.description: The number of times each type of prepared statement command has been issued.

mysql.query.client.count.description: The number of statements executed by the server. This includes only statements sent to the server by clients.

mysql.query.count.description: The number of statements executed by the server.

mysql.replica.sql_delay.description: The number of seconds that the replica must lag the source.

mysql.replica.time_behind_source.description: This field is an indication of how “late” the replica is.

mysql.row_locks.description: The number of InnoDB row locks.

mysql.row_operations.description: The number of InnoDB row operations.

mysql.statement_event.count.description: Summary of current and recent statement events.

mysql.statement_event.wait.time.description: The total wait time of the summarized timed events.

mysql.table.average_row_length.description: The average row length in bytes for a given table.

mysql.table.io.wait.count.description: The total count of I/O wait events for a table.

mysql.table.io.wait.time.description: The total time of I/O wait events for a table.

mysql.table.lock_wait.read.count.description: The total table lock wait read events.

mysql.table.lock_wait.read.time.description: The total table lock wait read events times.

mysql.table.lock_wait.write.count.description: The total table lock wait write events.

mysql.table.lock_wait.write.time.description: The total table lock wait write events times.

mysql.table.rows.description: The number of rows for a given table.

mysql.table.size.description: The table size in bytes for a given table.

mysql.table_open_cache.description: The number of hits, misses or overflows for open tables cache lookups.

mysql.tmp_resources.description: The number of created temporary resources.

mysql.uptime.description: The number of seconds that the server has been up.

components.description: Named pipes are only supported on Unix operating systems.

metadata.description: This receiver supports opening a Unix Named Pipe (aka FIFO), and reading logs from it.

schema.description: NamedPipeConfig defines configuration for the named_pipe receiver

components.description: Named pipes are only supported on Unix operating systems.

metadata.description: This receiver supports opening a Unix Named Pipe (aka FIFO), and reading logs from it.

schema.description: NamedPipeConfig defines configuration for the named_pipe receiver

components.description: This receiver gives OpenTelemetry users the capability of monitoring network traffic, and answer questions like:

metadata.description:
The Netflow Receiver can listen for [netflow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NetFlow),
[sflow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SFlow), and [ipfix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IP_Flow_Information_Export)
data and convert it to OpenTelemetry logs. The receiver is based on the
[goflow2](https://github.com/netsampler/goflow2) project.

schema.description: Config represents the receiver config settings within the collector's config.yaml

hostname.description: The hostname or IP address that the listener will bind to

port.description: The port that the listener will bind to

queue_size.description: The size of the queue that the listener will use This is a buffer that will hold flow messages before they are processed by a worker

scheme.description: The scheme defines the type of flow data that the listener will receive The scheme must be one of sflow, netflow, or flow

send_raw.description: SendRaw determines whether to send raw flow messages instead of parsing them

sockets.description: The number of sockets that the listener will use

workers.description: The number of workers that the listener will use to decode incoming flow messages By default it will be two times the number of sockets Ideally set this to the number of CPU cores

components.description: You must configure NGINX to expose status information by editing the NGINX configuration. Please see [ngx_http_stub_status_module](http://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_stub_status_module.html) for a guide to configuring the NGINX stats module `ngx_http_stub_status_module`.

metadata.description:
This receiver can fetch stats from a NGINX instance using the `ngx_http_stub_status_module` module's `status`
endpoint.

nginx.connections_accepted.description: The total number of accepted client connections

nginx.connections_current.description: The current number of nginx connections by state

nginx.connections_handled.description: The total number of handled connections. Generally, the parameter value is the same as nginx.connections_accepted unless some resource limits have been reached (for example, the worker_connections limit).

nginx.requests.description: Total number of requests made to the server since it started

components.description: The purpose of this receiver is to allow users to monitor metrics from NSX-T environments.

metadata.description:
This receiver fetches metrics important to run virtual networking using NSX-T. The receiver ingests metrics via the
[NSX Rest API](https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-NSX-Data-Center-for-vSphere/6.4/nsx_64_api.pdf).

device.id.description: The name of the network interface.

nsxt.node.id.description: The ID of the NSX Node.

nsxt.node.name.description: The name of the NSX Node.

class.description: The CPU usage of the architecture allocated for either DPDK (datapath) or non-DPDK (services) processes.

nsxt.node.cpu.utilization.description: The average amount of CPU being used by the node.

nsxt.node.filesystem.usage.description: The amount of storage space used by the node.

nsxt.node.filesystem.utilization.description: The percentage of storage space utilized.

nsxt.node.memory.cache.usage.description: The size of the node's memory cache.

nsxt.node.memory.usage.description: The memory usage of the node.

nsxt.node.network.io.description: The number of bytes which have flowed through the network interface.

nsxt.node.network.packet.count.description: The number of packets which have flowed through the network interface on the node.

schema.description: Config is the configuration for the NSX receiver

components.description: - `endpoint`: (default = `pool.ntp.org:123`) Endpoint of the NTP server. Must be formatted as `{host}:{port}`.

metadata.description: This receiver periodically retrieves the clock offset from a NTP server.

ntp.host.description: NTP server used. Corresponds to configured `host`.

ntp.offset.description: Time difference between local and NTP server clocks

schema.description: Config is the configuration for the NSX receiver

components.description: To use the Oracle DB receiver you must define how to connect to your DB. This can be done in two ways, defined in the [Primary](#primary-configuration-option) and [Secondary](#secondary-configuration-option) configuration option sections. Defining one of the two configurations is required. If both are defined, the primary option will be used.

metadata.description: This receiver periodically queries an Oracle Database host to collect metrics.

host.name.description: The host name of Oracle Server

oracledb.instance.name.description: The name of the instance that data is coming from.

service.instance.id.description: A unique identifier of the Oracle DB instance in the format host:port/serviceName. (defaults to 'unknown:1521', in case of error in generating this value)

client.address.description: Hostname or address of the client.

client.port.description: TCP port used by the client.

db.query.text.description: The text of the database query being executed.

db.server.name.description: The name of the server hosting the database.

db.system.name.description: The database management system (DBMS) product as identified by the client instrumentation.

network.peer.address.description: IP address of the peer client.

network.peer.port.description: TCP port used by the peer client.

oracledb.application_wait_time.description: The total time (in seconds) a query spent waiting on the application before it could proceed with execution (reporting delta).

oracledb.buffer_gets.description: Number of logical reads (i.e., buffer cache accesses) performed by a query (reporting delta).

oracledb.child_number.description: The child number of the query.

oracledb.cluster_wait_time.description: Total time (in seconds) that a query waited due to Oracle Real Application Clusters (RAC) coordination (reporting delta).

oracledb.concurrency_wait_time.description: Total time (in seconds) a query spent waiting on concurrency-related events (reporting delta).

oracledb.cpu_time.description: Total time (in seconds) that the CPU spent actively processing a query, excluding time spent waiting (reporting delta).

oracledb.direct_reads.description: The number of direct path reads performed by a query — i.e., data blocks read directly from disk into the session’s memory (reporting delta).

oracledb.direct_writes.description: The number of direct path write operations, where data is written directly to disk from user memory (reporting delta).

oracledb.disk_reads.description: The number of physical reads a query performs — that is, the number of data blocks read from disk (reporting delta).

oracledb.duration_sec.description: Total time taken by a database query to execute.

oracledb.elapsed_time.description: The total time (in seconds) taken by a query from start to finish, including CPU time and all types of waits (reporting delta).

oracledb.event.description: The specific wait event that a query or session is currently experiencing.

oracledb.executions.description: The number of times a specific SQL query has been executed (reporting delta).

oracledb.module.description: Logical module name of the client application that initiated a query or session.

oracledb.osuser.description: Name of the operating system user that initiated or is running the Oracle database session.

oracledb.physical_read_bytes.description: The total number of bytes read from disk by a query (reporting delta).

oracledb.physical_read_requests.description: The number of physical I/O read operations performed by a query (reporting delta).

oracledb.physical_write_bytes.description: The total number of bytes written to disk by a query (reporting delta).

oracledb.physical_write_requests.description: The number of times a query requested to write data to disk (reporting delta).

oracledb.plan_hash_value.description: Binary hash value calculated on the query execution plan and used to identify similar query execution plans, reported in the HEX format.

oracledb.procedure_execution_count.description: The number of times the stored procedure has been executed, derived from the minimum statement execution count across all statements in the procedure (reporting delta). Please note, this is best effort and may not be accurate in some scenarios. Use with caution.

oracledb.procedure_id.description: The identifier of the stored procedure or function being executed by the query.

oracledb.procedure_name.description: Name of the database object that a query is accessing.

oracledb.procedure_type.description: Type of the database object that a query is accessing.

oracledb.process.description: The operating system process ID (PID) associated with a session.

oracledb.program.description: Name of the client program or tool that initiated the Oracle database session.

oracledb.query_plan.description: The query execution plan used by the SQL Server.

oracledb.rows_processed.description: The total number of rows that a query has read, returned, or affected during its execution (reporting delta).

oracledb.schemaname.description: Oracle schema under which SQL statements are being executed

oracledb.serial.description: Serial number associated with a session.

oracledb.sid.description: ID of the Oracle Server session.

oracledb.sql_id.description: The SQL ID of the query.

oracledb.state.description: Current state of the query or the session executing it.

oracledb.status.description: Execution state or result of a database query or session.

oracledb.user_io_wait_time.description: The total time (in seconds) a query spent waiting for user I/O operations—such as reading or writing data to disk or network file systems (reporting delta).

oracledb.wait_class.description: The category of wait events a query or session is currently experiencing in Oracle Database.

user.name.description: Database user name under which a session is connected to

db.server.top_query.description: Collection of event metrics for top N queries, filtered based on the highest CPU time consumed (oracledb.elapsed_time).

oracledb.consistent_gets.description: Number of times a consistent read was requested for a block from the buffer cache.

oracledb.db_block_gets.description: Number of times a current block was requested from the buffer cache.

oracledb.ddl_statements_parallelized.description: Number of DDL statements that were executed in parallel

oracledb.dml_locks.limit.description: Maximum limit of active DML (Data Manipulation Language) locks, -1 if unlimited.

oracledb.dml_locks.usage.description: Current count of active DML (Data Manipulation Language) locks.

oracledb.dml_statements_parallelized.description: Number of DML statements that were executed in parallel

oracledb.enqueue_deadlocks.description: Total number of deadlocks between table or row locks in different sessions.

oracledb.enqueue_locks.limit.description: Maximum limit of active enqueue locks, -1 if unlimited.

oracledb.enqueue_locks.usage.description: Current count of active enqueue locks.

oracledb.enqueue_resources.limit.description: Maximum limit of active enqueue resources, -1 if unlimited.

oracledb.enqueue_resources.usage.description: Current count of active enqueue resources.

oracledb.exchange_deadlocks.description: Number of times that a process detected a potential deadlock when exchanging two buffers and raised an internal, restartable error. Index scans are the only operations that perform exchanges.

oracledb.executions.description: Total number of calls (user and recursive) that executed SQL statements

oracledb.parallel_operations_downgraded_.description: Number of times parallel execution was requested and the degree of parallelism was reduced down to 1-25% because of insufficient parallel execution servers
Number of times parallel execution was requested and the degree of parallelism was reduced down to 25-50% because of insufficient parallel execution servers
Number of times parallel execution was requested and the degree of parallelism was reduced down to 50-75% because of insufficient parallel execution servers
Number of times parallel execution was requested and the degree of parallelism was reduced down to 75-99% because of insufficient parallel execution servers
Number of times parallel execution was requested but execution was serial because of insufficient parallel execution servers

oracledb.parallel_operations_not_downgra.description: Number of times parallel execution was executed at the requested degree of parallelism

oracledb.physical_read_io_requests.description: Number of read requests for application activity

oracledb.physical_reads_direct.description: Number of reads directly from disk, bypassing the buffer cache

oracledb.physical_write_io_requests.description: Number of write requests for application activity

oracledb.physical_writes_direct.description: Number of writes directly to disk, bypassing the buffer cache

oracledb.processes.limit.description: Maximum limit of active processes, -1 if unlimited.

oracledb.queries_parallelized.description: Number of SELECT statements executed in parallel

oracledb.sessions.limit.description: Maximum limit of active sessions, -1 if unlimited.

oracledb.tablespace_size.limit.description: Maximum size of tablespace in bytes, -1 if unlimited.

oracledb.transactions.limit.description: Maximum limit of active transactions, -1 if unlimited.

oracledb.user_commits.description: Number of user commits. When a user commits a transaction, the redo generated that reflects the changes made to database blocks must be written to disk. Commits often represent the closest thing to a user transaction rate.

oracledb.user_rollbacks.description: Number of times users manually issue the ROLLBACK statement or an error occurs during a user's transactions

components.description: The receiver will watch the directory and read files. If a file is updated or added, the receiver will read it in its entirety again.

metadata.description:
This receiver will read pipeline data from JSON files. The data is written in
[Protobuf JSON
  encoding](https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/proto3#json)
using [OpenTelemetry
protocol](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-proto).

components.description: The OpenTelemetry Protocol with Apache Arrow receiver is an extension of the core OpenTelemetry Collector [OTLP receiver](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/tree/main/receiver/otlpreceiver) component with additional support for [OpenTelemetry Protocol with Apache Arrow](https://github.com/open-telemetry/otel-arrow).

metadata.description:
Receives telemetry data using [OpenTelemetry Protocol with Apache
Arrow](https://github.com/open-telemetry/otel-arrow) and standard
[OTLP](
https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/protocol/otlp.md)
protocol via gRPC.

request_limit_mib.description: RequestLimitMiB limits the number of requests that are received by the stream based on uncompressed request size. Request size is used to control how much traffic we admit for processing.  When this field is zero, admission control is disabled.

waiting_limit_mib.description: WaitingLimitMiB is the limit on the amount of data waiting to be consumed. This is a dimension of memory limiting to ensure waiters are not consuming an unexpectedly large amount of memory in the arrow receiver.

arrow_config.description: ArrowConfig support configuring the Arrow receiver.

admission_limit_mib.description: Deprecated: This field is no longer supported, use cfg.Admission.RequestLimitMiB instead.

memory_limit_mib.description: MemoryLimitMiB is the size of a shared memory region used by all Arrow streams, in MiB.  When too much load is passing through, they will see ResourceExhausted errors.

waiter_limit.description: Deprecated: This field is no longer supported, use cfg.Admission.WaiterLimit instead.

zstd.description: Zstd settings apply to OTel-Arrow use of gRPC specifically.

protocols.description: Protocols is the configuration for the supported protocols.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for OTel Arrow receiver.

admission.description: Admission is the configuration for controlling amount of request memory entering the receiver.

components.description: The following settings are required:

metadata.description:
The Podman Stats Receiver queries the Podman service API to fetch stats for all running containers
on a configured interval.  These stats are for container resource usage of cpu, memory, network, and
the [blkio controller](https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/cgroup-v1/blkio-controller.txt).

container.image.name.description: The name of the image in use by the container.

container.runtime.description: The runtime of the container. For this receiver, it will always be 'podman'.

core.description: The CPU core number when utilising per-CPU metrics.

container.blockio.io_service_bytes_recur.description: Number of bytes transferred from the disk by the container
Number of bytes transferred to the disk by the container

container.cpu.percent.description: Percent of CPU used by the container.

container.cpu.usage.percpu.description: Total CPU time consumed per CPU-core.

endpoint.description: The URL of the podman server.  Default is "unix:///run/podman/podman.sock"

components.description: See PostgreSQL documentation for [supported versions](https://www.postgresql.org/support/versioning).

metadata.description: This receiver queries the PostgreSQL [statistics collector](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/13/monitoring-stats.html).

feature_gates.description: Metric `postgresql.wal.lag` is replaced by more precise `postgresql.wal.delay`.
Use of connection pooling
Moves Schema Names into dedicated Attribute

postgresql.index.name.description: The name of the index on a table.

service.instance.id.description: A unique identifier of the PostgreSQL instance in the format host:port (defaults to 'unknown:5432' in case of error in generating this value).

bg_buffer_source.description: The source of a buffer write.

bg_duration_type.description: The type of time spent during the checkpoint.

db.namespace.description: The namespace or schema of the database where the query is executed.

db.query.text.description: The text of the database query being executed.

db.system.name.description: The database management system (DBMS) product as identified by the client instrumentation.

mode.description: Name of the lock mode held or desired by the process.

network.peer.address.description: IP address of the client connected to this backend.

network.peer.port.description: TCP port number that the client is using for communication with this backend.

postgresql.application_name.description: Name of the application that is connected to this backend.

postgresql.calls.description: Number of times the statement was executed, reported in delta value.

postgresql.client_hostname.description: Host name of the connected client, as reported by a reverse DNS lookup of client_addr.

postgresql.query_id.description: Identifier of this backend's most recent query. If state is active this field shows the identifier of the currently executing query. In all other states, it shows the identifier of last query that was executed.

postgresql.query_plan.description: The execution plan used by PostgreSQL for the query.

postgresql.query_start.description: Time when the currently active query was started, or if state is not active, when the last query was started.

postgresql.queryid.description: Hash code to identify identical normalized queries.

postgresql.rolname.description: The name of the PostgreSQL role that executed the query.

postgresql.rows.description: Total number of rows retrieved or affected by the statement, reported in delta value.

postgresql.shared_blks_dirtied.description: Total number of shared blocks dirtied by the statement, reported in delta value.

postgresql.shared_blks_hit.description: Total number of shared block cache hits by the statement, reported in delta value.

postgresql.shared_blks_read.description: Total number of shared blocks read by the statement, reported in delta value.

postgresql.shared_blks_written.description: Total number of shared blocks written by the statement, reported in delta value.

postgresql.state.description: Current overall state of this backend

postgresql.temp_blks_read.description: Total number of temp blocks read by the statement, reported in delta value.

postgresql.temp_blks_written.description: Total number of temp blocks written by the statement, reported in delta value.

postgresql.total_exec_time.description: Total time spent executing the statement, in delta milliseconds.

postgresql.total_plan_time.description: Total time spent planning the statement, in delta milliseconds.

postgresql.wait_event.description: Wait event name if backend is currently waiting, otherwise NULL.

postgresql.wait_event_type.description: The type of event for which the backend is waiting, if any; otherwise NULL.

relation.description: OID of the relation targeted by the lock, or null if the target is not a relation or part of a relation.

replication_client.description: The IP address of the client connected to this backend. If this field is "unix", it indicates either that the client is connected via a Unix socket.

user.name.description: Name of the user logged into this backend.

wal_operation_lag.description: The operation which is responsible for the lag.

postgresql.bgwriter.duration.description: Total time spent writing and syncing files to disk by checkpoints.

postgresql.bgwriter.maxwritten.description: Number of times the background writer stopped a cleaning scan because it had written too many buffers.

postgresql.blks_hit.description: Number of times disk blocks were found already in the buffer cache.

postgresql.blks_read.description: Number of disk blocks read in this database.

postgresql.connection.max.description: Configured maximum number of client connections allowed

postgresql.function.calls.description: The number of calls made to a function. Requires `track_functions=pl|all` in Postgres config.

postgresql.index.scans.description: The number of index scans on a table.

postgresql.index.size.description: The size of the index on disk.

postgresql.operations.description: The number of db row operations.

postgresql.replication.data_delay.description: The amount of data delayed in replication.

postgresql.rows.description: The number of rows in the database.

components.description: - `include` (required): The glob path for files to watch - `collection_interval` (default = `1m`): The interval at which metrics are emitted by this receiver. - `initial_delay` (default = `1s`): defines how long this receiver waits before starting. - `block_profile_fraction` (default = `1`): (self scraper only) Fraction of blocking events that are profiled. A value <= 0 disables profiling. See https://golang.org/pkg/runtime/#SetBlockProfileRate for details. - `mutex_profile_fraction` (default = `1`): (self scraper only) Fraction of mutex contention events that are profiled. A value <= 0 disables profiling. See https://golang.org/pkg/runtime/#SetMutexProfileFraction for details.

metadata.description: The Pprof Receiver collects profiles from files specified with a glob pattern, from a remote endpoint or the running collector.

schema.description: Config defines the configuration for the pprof receiver.

block_profile_fraction.description: Self-scraper only: Fraction of blocking events that are profiled. A value <= 0 disables profiling. See https://golang.org/pkg/runtime/#SetBlockProfileRate for details.

include.description: Include is the glob pattern for pprof files to scrape.

mutex_profile_fraction.description: Self-scraper only: Fraction of mutex contention events that are profiled. A value <= 0 disables profiling. See https://golang.org/pkg/runtime/#SetMutexProfileFraction for details.

components.description: See the [Design](DESIGN.md) for additional information on this receiver.

metadata.description: The Prometheus Receiver receives metric data in [Prometheus](https://prometheus.io/) format.

feature_gates.description: Enables the Prometheus created-timestamps-zero-injection feature. Created timestamps are injected as 0-valued samples when appropriate. This is disabled by default due to worse CPU performance with higher metric volumes.

Converts scraped Prometheus native histograms into OpenTelemetry exponential histograms. You still need to configure 'scrape_native_histograms: true' in your Prometheus scrape config to actually scrape native histograms. For mixed histograms (both classic and native), only the native histogram buckets are used.
When enabled, the `otel_scope_info` metric is ignored for scope attribute extraction.
When enabled, the Prometheus receiver will leave the start time unset. Use the metric_start_time processor instead if you need this functionality.
When enabled, the Prometheus receiver will retrieve the start time for Summary, Histogram and Sum metrics from _created metric.

prom_config.description: PromConfig is a redeclaration of promconfig.Config because we need custom unmarshaling as prometheus "config" uses `yaml` tags.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for Prometheus receiver.

api_server.description: APIServer has the settings to enable the receiver to host the Prometheus API server in agent mode. This allows the user to call the endpoint to get the config, service discovery, and targets for debugging purposes.

components.description: The [Prometheus Remote Write 2.0 protocol](https://prometheus.io/docs/specs/prw/remote_write_spec_2_0/) is still evolving. As the protocol specification changes, we update our implementation to match, which can affect compatibility with different Prometheus versions.

components.description: The following settings can be optionally configured: - `endpoint` (default = pulsar://localhost:6650): The url of pulsar cluster. - `topic` (default = otlp_spans for traces, otlp_metrics for metrics, otlp_logs for logs): The name of the pulsar topic to consume from. - `encoding` (default = otlp_proto): The encoding of the payload sent to pulsar. Available encodings: - `otlp_proto`: the payload is deserialized to `ExportTraceServiceRequest`. - `jaeger_proto`: the payload is deserialized to a single Jaeger proto `Span`. - `jaeger_json`: the payload is deserialized to a single Jaeger JSON Span using `jsonpb`.

topic.description: The topic of pulsar to consume logs,metrics,traces. (default = "otlp_traces" for traces, "otlp_metrics" for metrics, "otlp_logs" for logs)

components.description: To use the receiver creator, you must first configure one or more [observers](../../extension/observer/README.md) that will discover networked endpoints that you may be interested in. The configured rules will be evaluated for each endpoint discovered. If the rule evaluates to true then the receiver for that rule will be started against the matched endpoint.

components.description: The Redis INFO command returns information and statistics about a Redis server (see [https://redis.io/commands/info](https://redis.io/commands/info) for details). The Redis receiver extracts values from the result and converts them to open telemetry metrics. Details about the metrics produced by the Redis receiver can be found by browsing [metric_functions.go](metric_functions.go).

metadata.description:
The Redis Receiver is designed to retrieve Redis INFO data from a single Redis
instance, build metrics from that data, and send them to the next consumer at a
configurable interval.

written by SAP for connecting to SAP HANA with the golang sql module to execute several monitoring queries.

components.description: To get started with the Solace receiver, a telemetry queue and authentication details must be configured. If connecting to a broker other than localhost, the `broker` field should be configured. ```yaml receivers: solace: broker: [localhost:5671] auth: sasl_plain: username: otel password: otel01$ queue: queue://#telemetry-profile123

metadata.description: The Solace Receiver receives trace data from a [Solace PubSub+ Event Broker](https://solace.com/products/event-broker/).

solacereceiver_fatal_unmarshalling_error.description: Number of fatal message unmarshalling errors

solacereceiver_need_upgrade.description: Indicates with value 1 that receiver requires an upgrade and is not compatible with messages received from a broker

solacereceiver_receiver_flow_control_rec.description: Most recent/current retry count when flow controlled

solacereceiver_receiver_flow_control_sta.description: Indicates the flow control status of the receiver. 0 = not flow controlled, 1 = currently flow controlled

solacereceiver_receiver_flow_control_tot.description: Number of times the receiver instance became flow controlled

solacereceiver_receiver_flow_control_wit.description: Number of times the receiver instance became flow controlled and resolved situations after the first retry

solacereceiver_receiver_status.description: Indicates the status of the receiver as an enum. 0 = starting, 1 = connecting, 2 = connected, 3 = disabled (often paired with needs_upgrade), 4 = terminating, 5 = terminated

solacereceiver_recoverable_unmarshalling.description: Number of recoverable message unmarshalling errors

flow_control.description: FlowControl defines the configuration for what to do in backpressure scenarios, e.g. memorylimiter errors

flow_control_delayed_retry.description: FlowControlDelayedRetry represents the strategy of waiting for a defined amount of time (in time.Duration) and attempt redelivery

sasl_x_auth_2_config.description: SaslXAuth2Config defines the configuration for the SASL XAUTH2 authentication.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for Solace receiver.

broker.description: The list of solace brokers (default localhost:5671)

max_unacknowledged.description: The maximum number of unacknowledged messages the Solace broker can transmit, to configure AMQP Link

queue.description: The name of the solace queue to consume from, it is required parameter

components.description: **By default the Splunk Enterprise receiver is not configured to gather any metrics other than `splunk.health`**

metadata.description:
The Splunk Enterprise Receiver is a pull based tool which enables the ingestion of performance metrics describing the operational status of a user's Splunk Enterprise deployment to an appropriate observability tool.
It is designed to leverage several different data sources to gather these metrics including the [introspection api endpoint](https://help.splunk.com/en/splunk-enterprise/leverage-rest-apis/rest-api-reference/9.1/introspection-endpoints/introspection-endpoint-descriptions) and serializing
results from ad-hoc searches. Because of this, care must be taken by users when enabling metrics as running searches can effect your Splunk Enterprise Deployment and introspection may fail to report for Splunk
Cloud deployments. The primary purpose of this receiver is to empower those tasked with the maintenance and care of a Splunk Enterprise deployment to leverage opentelemetry and their observability toolset in their
jobs.

splunk.bucket.dir.description: The bucket super-directory (home, cold, thawed) for each index

splunk.feature.description: The Feature name from the Splunk Health Introspection Endpoint

splunk.feature.health.description: The Health (in color form) of a Splunk Feature from the Splunk Health Introspection Endpoint

splunk.host.description: The name of the splunk host

splunk.index.name.description: The name of the index reporting a specific KPI

splunk.indexer.searchable.description: The searchability status reported for a specific object

splunk.indexer.status.description: The status message reported for a specific object

splunk.kvstore.external.description: Value denoting if the KV store is using an external service

splunk.kvstore.status.value.description: The string value of the status returned when reporting on KV store using the introspection endpoint

splunk.kvstore.storage.engine.description: The backend storage used by the KV store

splunk.queue.name.description: The name of the queue reporting a specific KPI

splunk.rollingorrestart.description: Boolean that indicates if there is a rolling restart or rolling upgrade in progress.

splunk.search.state.description: The dispatch state of the search

splunk.searchable.restart.description: Boolean that indicates if a searchable rolling restart/upgrade in progress.t

splunk.splunkd.build.description: The build number for this Splunk instance version

splunk.aggregation.queue.ratio.description: Gauge tracking the average indexer aggregation queue ration (%). *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.buckets.searchable.status.description: Gauge tracking the number of buckets and their searchable status. *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.data.indexes.extended.bucket.even.description: Count of events in this bucket super-directory. *Note:** Must be pointed at specific indexer `endpoint`.

splunk.data.indexes.extended.bucket.hot..description: (If size > 0) Number of hot buckets. *Note:** Must be pointed at specific indexer `endpoint`.

splunk.data.indexes.extended.bucket.warm.description: (If size > 0) Number of warm buckets. *Note:** Must be pointed at specific indexer `endpoint` and gathers metrics from only that indexer.

splunk.data.indexes.extended.event.count.description: Count of events for index, excluding frozen events. Approximately equal to the event_count sum of all buckets. *Note:** Must be pointed at specific indexer `endpoint` and gathers metrics from only that indexer.

splunk.data.indexes.extended.raw.size.description: Size in bytes on disk of the <bucket>/rawdata/ directories of all buckets in this index, excluding frozen *Note:** Must be pointed at specific indexer `endpoint` and gathers metrics from only that indexer.

splunk.data.indexes.extended.total.size.description: Size in bytes on disk of this index *Note:** Must be pointed at specific indexer `endpoint` and gathers metrics from only that indexer.

splunk.health.description: The status ('red', 'yellow', or 'green') of the Splunk server. Health of 'red' produces a 0 while all other colors produce a 1.

splunk.indexer.avg.rate.description: Gauge tracking the average rate of indexed data. **Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.indexer.cpu.time.description: Gauge tracking the number of indexing process cpu seconds per instance

splunk.indexer.queue.ratio.description: Gauge tracking the average indexer index queue ration (%). *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.indexer.raw.write.time.description: Gauge tracking the number of raw write seconds per instance

splunk.indexer.rollingrestart.status.description: The status of a rolling restart.

splunk.indexer.throughput.description: Gauge tracking average bytes per second throughput of indexer. *Note:** Must be pointed at specific indexer `endpoint` and gathers metrics from only that indexer.

splunk.indexes.avg.size.description: Gauge tracking the indexes and their average size (gb). *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.indexes.avg.usage.description: Gauge tracking the indexes and their average usage (%). *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.indexes.bucket.count.description: Gauge tracking the indexes and their bucket counts. *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.indexes.median.data.age.description: Gauge tracking the indexes and their median data age (days). *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.indexes.size.description: Gauge tracking the indexes and their total size (gb). *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.io.avg.iops.description: Gauge tracking the average IOPs used per instance

splunk.kvstore.backup.status.description: Backup and restore status of the KV store.

splunk.kvstore.replication.status.description: Replication status of the KV store.

splunk.kvstore.status.description: This is the overall status of the kvstore for the given deployment.

splunk.license.expiration.seconds_remain.description: Gauge tracking the seconds remaining on any given Splunk License found via Splunk API. **Note:** This will only work on a Cluster Manager.

splunk.license.index.usage.description: Gauge tracking the indexed license usage per index

splunk.parse.queue.ratio.description: Gauge tracking the average indexer parser queue ration (%). *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.pipeline.set.count.description: Gauge tracking the number of pipeline sets per indexer. **Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

splunk.scheduler.avg.execution.latency.description: Gauge tracking the average execution latency of scheduled searches

splunk.scheduler.avg.run.time.description: Gauge tracking the average runtime of scheduled searches

splunk.scheduler.completion.ratio.description: Gauge tracking the ratio of completed to skipped scheduled searches

splunk.search.duration.description: Gauge tracking the duration in seconds of the last search probe call.

splunk.search.initiation.description: Gauge tracking whether the last search probe successfully initiated a search.

splunk.search.status.description: Gauge tracking the dispatch status of the last search probe.

splunk.search.success.description: Gauge tracking whether the last search probe call was successful with the dispatch state 'DONE'.

splunk.server.introspection.queues.curre.description: Gauge tracking current length of queue. *Note:** Must be pointed at specific indexer `endpoint` and gathers metrics from only that indexer.
Gauge tracking current bytes waiting in queue. *Note:** Must be pointed at specific indexer `endpoint` and gathers metrics from only that indexer.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.adhoc.description: Gauge tracking number of ad hoc search artifacts currently on disk. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.adhoc.size.description: Gauge total size (MB) of ad hoc search artifacts currently on disk. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.completed.description: Gauge tracking number of artifacts currently on disk that belong to finished searches. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.completed..description: Gauge total size (MB) of artifacts currently on disk that belong to finished searches. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.incomplete.description: Gauge tracking number of artifacts currently on disk that belong to unfinished/running searches. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

Gauge total size (MB) of artifacts currently on disk that belong to unfinished/running searches. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.invalid.description: Gauge tracking number of artifacts currently on disk that are not in a valid state, such as missing info.csv file, etc. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.job.cache..description: Gauge tracking number search artifacts metadata stored in memory, available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.
Gauge tracking, in megabytes, memory used to cache job status and job info of all search artifacts, available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.savedsearc.description: Gauge tracking, for the `splunk.server.searchartifacts.scheduled` number of scheduled search artifacts, how many different saved-searches they belong to. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.scheduled.description: Gauge tracking number of scheduled search artifacts currently on disk. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.server.searchartifacts.scheduled..description: Gauge total size (MB) of scheduled search artifacts currently on disk. Note:* Must be pointed at specific Search Head endpoint and gathers metrics from only that Search Head. Available in builds 9.1.2312.207+ and 9.3.x+.

splunk.typing.queue.ratio.description: Gauge tracking the average indexer typing queue ration (%). *Note:** Search is best run against a Cluster Manager.

components.description: The following settings are required:

metadata.description:
The Splunk HEC receiver accepts events in the [Splunk HEC
format](https://help.splunk.com/en/splunk-enterprise/get-data-in/get-started-with-getting-data-in/10.0/get-data-with-http-event-collector/format-events-for-http-event-collector).
This allows the collector to receive logs and metrics.
The collector accepts data formatted as JSON [HEC events](https://help.splunk.com/en/splunk-enterprise/get-data-in/get-started-with-getting-data-in/10.0/get-data-with-http-event-collector/format-events-for-http-event-collector#ariaid-title5)
under any path or as EOL separated log [raw data](https://help.splunk.com/en/splunk-enterprise/get-data-in/get-started-with-getting-data-in/10.0/get-data-with-http-event-collector/format-events-for-http-event-collector#raw-event-parsing-0)
if sent to the `raw_path` path.

ack.description: Ack defines configuration for the ACK functionality of the HEC receiver

extension.description: Extension defines the extension to use for acking of events. Without specifying an extension, the ACK endpoint won't be exposed

path.description: Path for Ack API, default is '/services/collector/ack'. Ignored if Extension is not provided.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for the Splunk HEC receiver.

health_path.description: HealthPath for health API, default is '/services/collector/health'

hec_metadata_to_otel_attrs.description: HecToOtelAttrs creates a mapping from HEC metadata to attributes.

raw_path.description: RawPath for raw data collection, default is '/services/collector/raw'

splitting.description: Splitting defines the splitting strategy used by the receiver when ingesting raw events. Can be set to "line" or "none". Default is "line".

components.description: For more about OTel/STEF, see [STEF exporter docs](../../exporter/stefexporter/README.md).

metadata.description: Receives data via gRPC in OTel/STEF format.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for STEF receiver.

components.description: **Connection Configuration (choose one approach):**

metadata.description: The SQL Query Receiver uses custom SQL queries to generate logs and/or metrics from a database connection.

max_open_conn.description: The maximumn number of open connections to the sql server. <= 0 means unlimited

components.description: Make sure to run the collector as administrator in order to collect all performance counters for metrics.

metadata.description:
The `sqlserver` receiver grabs metrics/logs about a Microsoft SQL Server instance. The receiver works by either using the
Windows Performance Counters, or by directly connecting to the instance and querying it. Windows Performance Counters
are only available when running on Windows.

host.name.description: The host name of SQL Server

service.instance.id.description: A unique identifier of the SQL Server instance in the format host:port. This resource attribute is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.computer.name.description: The name of the SQL Server instance being monitored.

sqlserver.database.name.description: The name of the SQL Server database.

sqlserver.instance.name.description: The name of the SQL Server instance being monitored.

client.address.description: Hostname or address of the client.

client.port.description: TCP port used by the client.

database.status.description: The current status of a database

db.query.text.description: The text of the database query being executed.

db.system.name.description: The database management system (DBMS) product as identified by the client instrumentation.

direction.description: The direction of flow of bytes or operations.

file_type.description: The type of file being monitored.

logical_filename.description: The logical filename of the file being monitored.

network.peer.address.description: IP address of the peer client.

network.peer.port.description: TCP port used by the peer client.

performance_counter.object_name.description: Category to which this counter belongs

physical_filename.description: The physical filename of the file being monitored.

replica.direction.description: The direction of flow of bytes for replica.

server.address.description: The network address of the server hosting the database.

server.port.description: The port number on which the server is listening.

sqlserver.blocking.start_time.description: Timestamp of when the current blocking wait began (ISO 8601 format).

sqlserver.blocking_session_id.description: Session ID that is blocking the current session. 0 if none.

sqlserver.context_info.description: Context information for the session, represented as a hexadecimal string.

sqlserver.cpu_time.description: CPU time consumed by the query, in seconds.

sqlserver.deadlock_priority.description: Deadlock priority value for the session.

sqlserver.estimated_completion_time.description: Estimated time remaining for the request to complete, in seconds.

sqlserver.execution_count.description: Number of times that the plan has been executed since it was last compiled, reported in delta value.

sqlserver.logical_reads.description: Number of logical reads (data read from cache/memory).

sqlserver.open_transaction_count.description: Number of transactions currently open in the session.

sqlserver.procedure_execution_count.description: Number of times that the procedure has been executed since it was last compiled, reported in delta value.

sqlserver.procedure_id.description: The SQL Server ID of the stored procedure, if any

sqlserver.procedure_name.description: The name of the stored procedure, if any

sqlserver.query_hash.description: Binary hash value calculated on the query and used to identify queries with similar logic, reported in the HEX format.

sqlserver.query_plan.description: The query execution plan used by the SQL Server.

sqlserver.query_plan_hash.description: Binary hash value calculated on the query execution plan and used to identify similar query execution plans, reported in the HEX format.

sqlserver.query_start.description: Timestamp of when the SQL query started (ISO 8601 format).

sqlserver.reads.description: Number of physical reads performed by the query.

sqlserver.request_status.description: Status of the request (e.g., running, suspended).

sqlserver.row_count.description: Number of rows affected or returned by the query.

sqlserver.session_id.description: ID of the SQL Server session.

sqlserver.session_status.description: Status of the session (e.g., running, sleeping).

sqlserver.total_elapsed_time.description: Total elapsed time for completed executions of this plan, reported in delta seconds.

sqlserver.total_grant_kb.description: The total amount of reserved memory grant in KB this plan received since it was compiled, reported in delta value.

sqlserver.total_logical_reads.description: Total number of logical reads performed by executions of this plan since it was compiled, reported in delta value.

sqlserver.total_logical_writes.description: Total number of logical writes performed by executions of this plan since it was compiled, reported in delta value.

sqlserver.total_physical_reads.description: Total number of physical reads performed by executions of this plan since it was compiled, reported in delta value.

sqlserver.total_rows.description: Total number of rows returned by the query, reported in delta value.

sqlserver.total_worker_time.description: Total amount of CPU time that was consumed by executions of this plan since it was compiled, reported in delta seconds.

sqlserver.transaction_id.description: Unique ID of the active transaction.

sqlserver.transaction_isolation_level.description: Transaction isolation level used in the session. Represented as numeric constant.

sqlserver.wait.resource.id.description: SQL Server identifier for the locked or waited-on resource, if available.

sqlserver.wait.resource.type.description: SQL Server type of the locked or waited-on resource, if available.

sqlserver.wait_resource.description: The resource for which the session is waiting.

sqlserver.wait_time.description: Duration in seconds the request has been waiting.

sqlserver.wait_type.description: Type of wait encountered by the request. Empty if none.

sqlserver.writes.description: Number of writes performed by the query.

tempdb.state.description: The status of the tempdb space usage.

user.name.description: Login name associated with the SQL Server session.

wait.category.description: Category of the reason for a wait.

wait.type.description: Type of the wait, view [WaitTypes documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/system-dynamic-management-views/sys-dm-os-wait-stats-transact-sql?view=sql-server-ver16#WaitTypes) for more information.

sqlserver.batch.request.rate.description: Number of batch requests received by SQL Server.

sqlserver.database.count.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.database.full_scan.rate.description: The number of unrestricted full table or index scans.

sqlserver.database.io.description: The number of bytes of I/O on this file.
sqlserver.database.io.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.database.latency.description: Total time that the users waited for I/O issued on this file.
sqlserver.database.latency.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.database.operations.description: The number of operations issued on the file.
sqlserver.database.operations.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.database.tempdb.space.description: Total free space in temporary DB.

sqlserver.lock.wait.count.description: Cumulative count of lock waits that occurred.
sqlserver.lock.wait.count.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.lock.wait.rate.description: Number of lock requests resulting in a wait.

sqlserver.lock.wait_time.avg.description: Average wait time for all lock requests that had to wait.
sqlserver.lock.wait_time.avg.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.memory.grants.pending.count.description: Total number of memory grants pending.

sqlserver.os.wait.duration.description: Total wait time for this wait type
sqlserver.os.wait.duration.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.page.buffer_cache.hit_ratio.description: Pages found in the buffer pool without having to read from disk.

sqlserver.page.checkpoint.flush.rate.description: Number of pages flushed by operations requiring dirty pages to be flushed.
sqlserver.page.checkpoint.flush.rate.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.page.lazy_write.rate.description: Number of lazy writes moving dirty pages to disk.
sqlserver.page.lazy_write.rate.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.page.life_expectancy.description: Time a page will stay in the buffer pool.

sqlserver.page.operation.rate.description: Number of physical database page operations issued.
sqlserver.page.operation.rate.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.page.split.rate.description: Number of pages split as a result of overflowing index pages.
sqlserver.page.split.rate.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.processes.blocked.description: The number of processes that are currently blocked
sqlserver.processes.blocked.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.resource_pool.disk.operations.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.resource_pool.disk.throttled.r.description: The number of read operations that were throttled in the last second
sqlserver.resource_pool.disk.throttled.r.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.resource_pool.disk.throttled.w.description: The number of write operations that were throttled in the last second
sqlserver.resource_pool.disk.throttled.w.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when the receiver is configured to directly connect to SQL Server.

sqlserver.transaction.mirror_write.rate.description: Total number of mirror write transactions.

sqlserver.transaction.rate.description: Number of transactions started for the database (not including XTP-only transactions).
sqlserver.transaction.rate.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.transaction.write.rate.description: Number of transactions that wrote to the database and committed.
sqlserver.transaction.write.rate.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.transaction_log.flush.data.rat.description: Total number of log bytes flushed.
sqlserver.transaction_log.flush.data.rat.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.transaction_log.flush.rate.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.transaction_log.flush.wait.rat.description: Number of commits waiting for a transaction log flush.
sqlserver.transaction_log.flush.wait.rat.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.transaction_log.growth.count.description: Total number of transaction log expansions for a database.
sqlserver.transaction_log.growth.count.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.transaction_log.shrink.count.description: Total number of transaction log shrinks for a database.
sqlserver.transaction_log.shrink.count.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.transaction_log.usage.description: Percent of transaction log space used.
sqlserver.transaction_log.usage.extended_documentation: This metric is only available when running on Windows.

sqlserver.user.connection.count.description: Number of users connected to the SQL Server.

feature_gates.description: When enabled, the server.address and server.port resource attributes are removed from metrics.

lookback_time.description: Enabled enables the collection of the top queries by the execution time. It will collect the top N queries based on totalElapsedTimeDiffs during the last collection interval. The query statement will also be reported, hence, it is not ideal to send it as a metric. Hence we are reporting them as logs. The `N` is configured via `TopQueryCount`

schema.description: Config defines configuration for a sqlserver receiver.

components.description: If `ignore_host_key` is not set then host key validation requires the agent either have a known_hosts file at a path specified by setting `known_hosts` or at default paths indicated by ssh man pages: $HOME/.ssh/known_hosts or /etc/ssh/known_hosts.

metadata.description: This receiver creates stats by connecting to an SSH server which may be an SFTP server.

sshcheck.duration.description: Measures the duration of SSH connection.

sshcheck.error.description: Records errors occurring during SSH check.

sshcheck.sftp_error.description: Records errors occurring during SFTP check.

sshcheck.sftp_status.description: 1 if the SFTP server replied to request, otherwise 0.

sshcheck.status.description: 1 if the SSH client successfully connected, otherwise 0.

components.description: The Following settings are optional:

metadata.description:
StatsD receiver for ingesting StatsD messages(https://github.com/statsd/statsd/blob/master/docs/metric_types.md) into
the OpenTelemetry Collector. Note: This receiver does not support horizontally scaled collector deployments. It is
intended to run in agent mode, where a single collector instance receives StatsD input.

schema.description: Config defines configuration for StatsD receiver.

counter_type.description: CounterType specifies how counter values are represented in exported metrics. Valid values: "int" (default), "float", "stochastic_int".

socket_permissions.description: Will only be used when transport set to 'unixgram'.

metadata.description: Parses Syslogs received over TCP or UDP.

schema.description: SysLogConfig defines configuration for the syslog receiver

components.description: This scraper generates a metric with a label for service state with a value of `1` if the unit is in that state. For example, the following metrics will be generated if the `nginx` service is currently active:

metadata.description: The Systemd Receiver gathers metrics for locally running systemd units.

cpu.mode.description: Breakdown of CPU usage by type.

systemd.unit.active_state.description: The active state of the unit (https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/systemd.html#Units)

systemd.service.cpu.time.description: Total CPU time spent by this service.

systemd.service.restarts.description: Number of automatic restarts for the service.
systemd.service.restarts.extended_documentation:
This exposes services' `NRestarts` property as a metric. This only tracks
automatic service restarts (restarts when the process exits), and does
not include manual restarts (e.g. from `systemctl restart`).

systemd.unit.state.description: 1 if the check resulted in active_state matching the current state, otherwise 0.

components.description: The following settings are required: - `endpoint`

metadata.description: This receiver creates stats by connecting to a TCP server.

tcpcheck.duration.description: Measures the duration of TCP connection.

tcpcheck.error.description: Records errors occurring during TCP check.

tcpcheck.status.description: 1 if the TCP client successfully connected, otherwise 0.

Protects against reading large amounts of data into memory | | `listen_address` | required | A listen address of the form `<ip>:<port>` |

components.description: By default, the TLS Check Receiver will emit a single metric, `tlscheck.time_left`, per target. This is measured in seconds until the date and time specified in the `NotAfter` field of the x.509 certificate. After certificate expiration, the metric value will be a negative integer measuring the time in seconds since expiry.

metadata.description: This receiver emits metrics about x.509 certificates.

tlscheck.target.description: Endpoint or file path at which the certificate was accessed.

tlscheck.x509.cn.description: The commonName in the subject of the certificate.

tlscheck.x509.issuer.description: The entity that issued the certificate.

tlscheck.x509.san.description: The Subject Alternative Name of the certificate.

tlscheck.time_left.description: Time in seconds until certificate expiry, as specified by `NotAfter` field in the x.509 certificate. Negative values represent time in seconds since expiration.

certificate_target.description: CertificateTarget represents a target for certificate checking, which can be either a network endpoint or a local file

file_format.description: FileFormat represents the format of a local certificate file.

schema.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

After certificate expiration, the metric value will be a negative integer measuring the time in seconds since expiry.
